name: Ensure security questions are registered in the AWS account
body:
### Security intent

This control validates that **security questions are registered in the AWS account** across AWS.

### Access condition reviewed

The assessment focuses on security questions that are not registered in the AWS account. It reviews the identity, credential, role, or policy state that creates the access condition described by the control.

### Practitioner note

Identity findings deserve careful cleanup rather than blind deletion. Confirm ownership and business need, remove unnecessary standing access, and prefer federation, groups, roles, MFA, and short-lived credentials over direct long-lived grants.

This control maps to a benchmark-style requirement, so the expected state should match the benchmark wording unless your organization has an approved exception.
description: Security questions that are not registered in the AWS account
remediationDescription:
## AWS remediation
+
+### Console
+1. Sign in as the AWS account root user.
+2. Open My Security Credentials.
+3. In Account settings, configure security challenge questions.
+4. Store answers in an approved secrets management process.
+5. Review after ownership/team changes.
+
+### Notes
+- There is no equivalent AWS CLI command for setting security challenge questions.
+- This action is root-account and console-driven.
+
+### References
+- https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_root-user.html
+- https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_root-user_manage.html
